title: Code of Conduct
text: AutoXplainR committed respectful, harassment-free community. considerate, critique ideas evidence rather people, disclose relevant conflicts interest, make space participants different levels experience. Unacceptable conduct includes harassment, discriminatory language, threats, doxxing, sustained disruption, publishing another person’s private information without permission. Report concerns privately Matteo Mazzarelli matteo.mazzarelli@gmail.com. Reports handled confidentially promptly practical. Maintainers may edit remove content, issue warnings, temporarily restrict participation, permanently exclude participants needed protect community.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: Thanks helping improve explanation reliability R. Contributions welcome issues, design discussions, documentation, tests, code.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: material API statistical-method change, open issue first. Describe: user claim method intended support; estimand assumptions; failure modes dependence, finite samples, model multiplicity; proposed object contract backward-compatibility impact; validation evidence simulation plan. Features accepted solely produce attractive chart. Changes must also preserve beginner-first workflow progressive disclosure rules PRODUCT.md.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: ordinary suite must start Java, access internet, open browser, require credentials. H2O tests isolated opt-:
code: pak::pak(c("devtools", "testthat", "roxygen2", "lintr")) devtools::load_all() devtools::test() devtools::check() AUTOXPLAIN_RUN_H2O=true Rscript -e 'devtools::test(filter = "h2o")'

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: Add unit tests happy paths, invalid inputs, statistical edge cases. Add deterministic simulation numerical method introduced. Preserve caller’s random-number state stochastic functions. silently drop columns, rows, factor levels. State whether intervals Monte Carlo, bootstrap, inferential. Add NEWS user documentation visible changes. Run devtools::document(), devtools::test(), devtools::check(). Keep measured statement coverage enforced 80% project floor; new statistical branches covered substantially deeply. Keep optional packages behind requireNamespace() checks. contributions licensed project MIT license. Participation subject Code Conduct.

title: Governance
text: AutoXplainR currently maintainer-led. maintainer final responsibility releases, CRAN submissions, security responses, statistical scope, API stability. Material method changes require public design issue evidence appropriate claim: literature references, simulations, cross-package comparisons, three. second reviewer approve changes statistical estimators, remote-data boundaries, evidence-grade thresholds stable 1.0 release. Decisions favor explicit assumptions, reproducibility, honest limitations feature count. Disagreements recorded relevant issue pull request.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: AutoXplainR helps person fitting one first tabular models answer four questions without needing assemble modeling stack: kind prediction problem ? well fitted model work data train ? patterns model use, reliable explanations? can communicate result without overstating ? package make safe path easy path. explanation evidence audit internal quality layer advanced view; first concept new user must understand.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: primary workflow must accept data frame target name, : detect regression, binary classification, multiclass classification; create reproducible held-evaluation split one supplied; fit simple baseline understandable primary model without requiring Java cloud account; compare using task-appropriate metrics plain-language definitions; compute global importance feature effects limitations attached; produce one self-contained, accessible report; optionally add LLM narrative subordinate computed results. next step remains one explicit argument away: model_set = "comparison" adds small, local candidate set displays performance-versus-complexity trade-offs without silently changing pre-specified primary model. Advanced users may supply split, model, prediction function, H2O AutoML run, explanation configuration, narrative provider.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: result support three levels detail: Guided: fitted, whether beats simple baseline, important patterns, concrete cautions. Compare: visible candidate performance, complexity, Pareto status, warning held-ranks descriptive rather free tuning loop. Evidence: permutation variability, dependence, supplied-model multiplicity, ALE/PDP support, provenance. Developer: repeat-level values, prediction contracts, recipes, metrics, prompts, machine-readable configuration. Every technical term guided report needs adjacent definition. Grades warnings must explain action user take next.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: Narratives never receive raw rows, fitted model objects, secrets, case-level predictions. Remote use explicit; package never discovers key sends data merely key exists. Provider order reflects July 2026 public offerings must remain configurable model availability changes: provider adapters must share one prompt contract, expose provider resolved model provenance, time , handle rate limits, fall back deterministic report without losing analysis.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: AutoXplainR automate causal claims, fairness certification, deployment approval. hide train/evaluation split select metrics without explaining . require LLM produce complete report. make users understand Rashomon sets can evaluate first model.

title: AutoXplainR Product Contract
text: release ready unless first-time user can complete primary workflow README, understand first screen report, obtain numerical conclusions remote narrative generation disabled.

title: NA
text: Fast unit tests added updated Numerical/simulation test added applicable Invalid input edge cases tested Optional integration remains isolated devtools::document(), devtools::test(), devtools::check() pass User-facing changes recorded NEWS

title: AutoXplainR release checklist
text: Releases cut clean main branch required GitHub Actions checks pass. maintainer owns final statistical-language CRAN-policy review; automation supporting evidence, substitute review.

title: AutoXplainR release checklist
text: Confirm version release date DESCRIPTION, NEWS.md, CITATION.cff, cran-comments.md. Confirm README vignette examples still express beginner-first product contract PRODUCT.md. Review user-facing metrics, warnings, narrative prompts unsupported causal, fairness, certainty claims. Run devtools::document() ensure generated files committed. Run spelling::spell_check_package() urlchecker::url_check(). Run lintr::lint_package(), testthat::test_local(), covr::package_coverage(); statement coverage must remain least 80%. Run devtools::check(args = "---cran") explain every remaining note cran-comments.md. Run isolated real-H2O test AUTOXPLAIN_RUN_H2O=true.

title: AutoXplainR release checklist
text: Require successful R CMD checks Ubuntu (devel, release, oldrel), Windows, macOS. Require successful lint, spelling, link, coverage, pkgdown, H2O jobs. Open deployed pkgdown site smoke-test beginner workflow narrow mobile viewport well desktop. Verify workflow log, fixture, report contains API key raw private data.

title: AutoXplainR release checklist
text: Create push signed version tag v<DESCRIPTION version>. Inspect workflow-built source package SHA-256 checksum. Review publish draft GitHub release created release workflow. Submit exact checked source archive CRAN record reviewer feedback cran-comments.md. acceptance, verify CRAN installation, pkgdown site, citations, release links clean R library. reuse move published version tag. Corrections require new package version new tag.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: 0.2 architecture establishes beginner-first fit, evaluate, explain, communicate workflow backed model-agnostic evidence audit. roadmap ordered user scientific leverage, novelty theatre.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Guided base-R fitting held-evaluation Java requirement. Plain-language model report progressive evidence disclosure. Explicit Gemini, Groq, Ollama, OpenRouter, local narrative providers. Beginner evaluation diagnostics: error summaries, confusion tables, probability scores, sample-size cautions, baseline-failure warnings. Optional local comparison set multiple understandable model complexities, measured resource dimensions, labeled Pareto frontier. Model-agnostic prediction contract H2O boundary. Repeated grouped permutation importance retained draws. ALE, diagnosed PDP, feature association, support outputs. Supplied near-optimal-model explanation prediction agreement. Evidence-first standalone report provenance. Fast core tests, isolated H2O tests, enforced coverage, CI, pkgdown, project policy. Version-checked source-package artifacts draft GitHub releases version tags.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Observation-level loss deltas explicitly supported inferential procedures multiplicity correction; benchmark xplainfi. Conditional-sampler interface rather treating blocked permutations general conditional estimator. Bootstrap observations refits separate permutation, evaluation-sample, model-fitting variability. Cross-fitted audit orchestration prevents training/evaluation leakage construction. Calibration, subgroup performance, missingness-shift diagnostics context explanation claims—fairness certification. Simulation corpus known ground truth, correlated features, interactions, null predictors, extrapolation, label noise.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: First-class guided candidate-set mode shares ordinary result, explainer, audit, visualization, report contracts. First-class Rashomon-set provider interface AutoML, resampling, regularization paths, user-defined candidate generators. Rashomon ALE bands class-reliance ranges coverage diagnostics. Case-level prediction ambiguity, feature-attribution disagreement, decision-threshold sensitivity. Candidate-relative Pareto selection held-performance model size without hiding trade-offs one weighted score. Extend Pareto objectives calibration, latency, stability, explanation agreement missing-objective diagnostics.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Versioned JSON audit schema validation package CI/model registry gates. Signed report manifests model, data, code, environment hashes. Redaction policy pluggable local narrative models. WCAG audit, print/PDF stylesheet, report localization. Reproducible benchmark website comparing runtime numerical agreement DALEX, ingredients, iml, xplainfi, reference implementations.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Stable public object schemas documented deprecation policy. Zero errors, warnings, unexplained notes across CRAN reference platforms. Independent review estimands, simulations, reporting language. least two non-H2O production adapters validated integration tests. Published methods/validation manuscript archived benchmark artifacts.

title: Security Policy
text: Security fixes applied current development branch recent released minor version.

title: Security Policy
text: open public issue suspected vulnerability accidental secret exposure. Email Matteo Mazzarelli matteo.mazzarelli@gmail.com : affected version platform; reproduction steps minimal example; potential impact; suggested mitigation. Please omit real API keys, personal data, proprietary model artifacts. receive acknowledgement within seven days.

title: Security Policy
text: Core explanation audits local access network. Remote narrative generation optional sends aggregated diagnostics. Users remain responsible provider terms data policy. API keys read arguments environment variables must never written reports, logs, fixtures, error messages. Generated HTML escapes model labels, feature names, findings, metadata.

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: Fitting model one part useful analysis. first-time modeler also needs know whether model works unseen rows, whether improves simple guess, fitted patterns matter, describe patterns without claiming analysis established. AutoXplainR puts questions one workflow. safe default local requires neither Java API account. vignette uses small built-mtcars data every step reproducible.

what: Fit and evaluate in one call
title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: mpg numeric two distinct values, recognized regression problem. AutoXplainR reproducibly holds 20% rows, learns preprocessing remaining rows, fits two models: linear regression using input features; intercept-baseline ignores input features. baseline matters. answers whether fitted relationships improve simple reference rather merely producing predictions.
code: result <- autoxplain(   mtcars,   target_column = "mpg",   seed = 2026 )  result #> <AutoXplainR guided result> #>   question:   predict `mpg` (regression) #>   engine:     base #>   data:       26 training + 6 evaluation rows #>   models:     2 (primary + baseline) #>   result:     primary model has rmse = 3.5529 #>   baseline:   30.3% improvement in rmse #>   next:       use as_explainers() to investigate the fitted patterns

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: regression, RMSE primary comparison metric. typical error outcome’s units, larger errors weighted heavily. Lower better. MAE average absolute error. R-squared compares squared error held-mean reference can negative genuinely unseen data. exact values six-row mtcars holdout basis scientific conclusions; example demonstrates contract. Real analyses need evaluation set large representative enough intended use.
code: result$leaderboard #>   rank        model_id                   model     role     rmse      mae #> 1    1      main_model       linear regression  primary 3.552918 2.797996 #> 2    2 simple_baseline intercept-only baseline baseline 5.096266 3.907692 #>    r_squared training_time_ms model_size_kb complexity fit_warning #> 1 -0.1281933                1      44.43750         11             #> 2 -1.3212249                2      19.82812          1             #>   prediction_time_ms #> 1                  1 #> 2                  0 result$evaluation$metric_definitions #>                                                                                                rmse  #>            "Typical prediction error, with larger mistakes weighted more heavily; lower is better."  #>                                                                                                 mae  #>                         "Average absolute prediction error in the target's units; lower is better."  #>                                                                                           r_squared  #> "Share of held-out variation explained relative to predicting the held-out mean; higher is better." result$evaluation$improvement_over_baseline #> [1] 0.3028389

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: standalone report uses progressive disclosure: modeling question baseline verdict; every held-metric definitions; input reliance fitted effect directions; warnings concrete next actions; collapsed technical evidence audit complete provenance. report need LLM, Plotly, H2O, browser runtime written.
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") render_model_report(result, path, top_features = 4, n_repeats = 5) file.exists(path) #> [1] TRUE unlink(path)

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: first call stays intentionally simple. comparing approachable model shapes help, use explicit comparison mode: adds shallow tree flexible tree pre-specified statistical model intercept-baseline. Pareto status asks whether another supplied model least good held-performance model size, strict improvement one. comparison descriptive. AutoXplainR silently promote holdout winner primary, selecting holdout reporting score final performance optimistic. Use fresh validation pre-specified resampling procedure model selection goal. interactive view available Plotly installed:
code: comparison <- autoxplain(   iris,   target_column = "Sepal.Length",   model_set = "comparison",   test_fraction = 0.4,   seed = 2026 )  model_tradeoffs(comparison) #> <AutoXplainR model trade-offs> #>   performance: rmse (lower is better) #>   complexity:  model_size_kb (lower is better) #>   Pareto set:  3 / 4 supplied models #>         model_id                   model      role      rmse model_size_kb #>       main_model       linear regression   primary 0.2963068      49.46094 #>       small_tree     small decision tree candidate 0.3744086      36.66406 #>  simple_baseline intercept-only baseline  baseline 0.8691950      32.51562 #>    flexible_tree  flexible decision tree candidate 0.4126230      43.25000 #>  pareto_optimal #>            TRUE #>            TRUE #>            TRUE #>           FALSE #>   note: Pareto status compares only the supplied models on the supplied evaluation data; it is not a final model-selection rule. plot_model_comparison(comparison)

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: guided result can converted model-agnostic explainer. Select primary model rather simple baseline: Repeated permutation importance measures much held-performance changes input shuffled: result keeps every repeat, Monte Carlo interval, sign stability. interval describes randomness shuffling. confidence interval population support causal claim. explain_effect() defaults accumulated local effects (ALE) numeric inputs: ALE summarizes local prediction changes inside observed feature bins. reduces direct extrapolation problem marginal partial-dependence plot dependent inputs, still describes fitted prediction function—effect intervening vehicle weight.

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: evidence audit available technical review useful: audit checks computation stability, feature association, supplied-model performance, explanation agreement. example supplies primary model baseline, can also demonstrate competitively performing supplied models influence explanation comparison. –D grade triage heuristic. p-value, certification, fairness test, safety assessment.
code: audit <- audit_explanations(   as_explainers(result),   features = c("wt", "hp", "disp"),   n_repeats = 10,   seed = 2026 )  audit #> <AutoXplainR explanation evidence audit> #>   grade:              C (diagnostic, not certification) #>   models:             2 (1 near-optimal) #>   stable claims:      16.7% #>   max dependence:     0.829 #>   explanation accord: n/a (one model) #>   prediction accord:  n/a (one model) #>  #> Findings #>   [warning] 2 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #>   [warning] 5 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported. #>   [note] The permutation budget is suitable for smoke testing, not a final report.

what: Add a narrative only if it helps communication
title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: deterministic provider works offline: Remote use requires explicit provider: aggregated diagnostics placed prompt. Raw rows, fitted objects, case-level predictions, secrets excluded. Provider model details attached returned text prose can audited. Generated prose still needs human review numerical report.
code: memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result) cat(substr(memo, 1, 400), "...") #> # Model Fit and Evaluation Report #>  #> ## Scope #> This is a descriptive summary of a regression task for `mpg`, covering 2 model(s) and 10 feature(s). #>  #> ## Did the model improve on a simple baseline? #> The linear regression was evaluated on 6 held-out test rows. Its **rmse** was 3.5529. #> That is a 30.3% improvement over the intercept-only baseline (5.0963). #>  #> ## What the main metric means #> **rmse:** Typical p ... attr(memo, "narrative_provenance") #> $provider_requested #> [1] "local" #>  #> $provider_used #> [1] "local" #>  #> $model #> NULL #>  #> $remote #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $fallback #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $disclosure #> [1] "Aggregated diagnostics only. No raw rows, fitted model objects, case-level predictions, or secrets are included." #>  #> $error #> NULL Sys.setenv(GEMINI_API_KEY = "...") memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "gemini")  # Or use a model running locally through Ollama memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "ollama")

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: Classification splits stratified. Binary tasks report log loss, Brier score, accuracy, balanced accuracy, ROC AUC. Multiclass tasks report log loss, Brier score, accuracy, macro recall. Log loss primary evaluates probability quality penalizes confident wrong predictions.
code: flowers <- autoxplain(iris, target_column = "Species", seed = 2026) flowers #> <AutoXplainR guided result> #>   question:   predict `Species` (multiclass) #>   engine:     base #>   data:       120 training + 30 evaluation rows #>   models:     2 (primary + baseline) #>   result:     primary model has log_loss = 1.1513 #>   baseline:   -4.8% improvement in log_loss #>   next:       use as_explainers() to investigate the fitted patterns flowers$evaluation$metric_definitions #>                                                                        log_loss  #>    "Probability error that penalizes confident wrong answers; lower is better."  #>                                                                     brier_score  #>                           "Average squared probability error; lower is better."  #>                                                                        accuracy  #>       "Share of held-out rows assigned to the correct class; higher is better."  #>                                                                    macro_recall  #> "Recall calculated for each class and then averaged equally; higher is better."

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: explanation layer depend guided fitting engine. Wrap ordinary R model directly: frameworks can supply predict_function. H2O AutoML remains explicit optional fitting engine autoxplain(..., engine = "h2o").
code: train <- mtcars[1:24, ] test <- mtcars[25:32, ] fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = train)  existing <- explain_model(   fit,   test,   y = "mpg",   label = "existing linear model",   metadata = list(evaluation_role = "test") )

title: Your First Model with AutoXplainR
text: Reasonable language specific fitted model evaluation data: held-rows, shuffling wt changed fitted model’s RMSE reported amount, displayed variation across permutation repeats. following requires different design evidence: Reducing vehicle weight cause fuel economy improve fitted ALE amount target population. AutoXplainR makes first statement easier compute preserve. turn second.

what: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: Fit. Check. Compare. Understand. Explain. AutoXplainR helps someone fitting one first tabular models move data frame responsible, shareable explanation. creates held-test set, compares understandable model simple baseline, defines every metric, investigates fitted patterns, tells user evidence support. default path local needs Java, cloud account, LLM. comparison mode adds multiple approachable candidates Pareto trade-offs. Advanced users can bring fitted model, use H2O AutoML, opt local hosted language models. Get started · Function reference · Product contract · Roadmap complete first screen: question, unseen-data result, baseline, evaluation size. scrolling inside image.

title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: default call fits two deliberately understandable references: linear, logistic, multinomial logistic primary model; intercept-baseline ignores every input. evaluated rows kept fitting. printed result answers first useful question—model improve simple guess?—package asks anyone interpret feature importance.
code: library(AutoXplainR)  result <- autoxplain(mtcars, target_column = "mpg", seed = 2026) result render_model_report(result, "my-model-report.html") <AutoXplainR guided result>   question:   predict `mpg` (regression)   engine:     base   models:     2 (primary + baseline)   result:     primary model has rmse = ...   baseline:   ...% improvement in rmse

what: Compare models without hiding the trade-off
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: Comparison scope. first-class, opt-extension beginner workflow: local comparison set contains pre-specified statistical model, small decision tree, flexible tree, simple baseline. AutoXplainR shows held-performance, fit prediction time, model size, complexity, candidate-relative Pareto frontier. collapse dimensions mysterious score. also silently replace primary model whichever candidate happened win holdout: reporting holdout score final performance optimistic. Performance versus model size, interpretation rule full candidate table kept frame.
code: comparison <- autoxplain(   iris,   target_column = "Sepal.Length",   model_set = "comparison",   test_fraction = 0.4,   seed = 2026 )  model_tradeoffs(comparison) plot_model_comparison(comparison)  # optional interactive Plotly view render_model_report(comparison, "comparison-report.html")

what: One report, from result to responsible language
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: Every screenshot crop package’s dependency-free HTML report, separate design mock-. report remains readable shared; require R, H2O, Plotly, LLM viewing time.

what: Open the analysis when you need more control
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: guided result converts ordinary model-agnostic explainers: Permutation intervals describe variation across random shuffles; population confidence intervals. ALE PDP describe fitted prediction function; establish effect intervening input. multi-model reliability review: audit retains repeat-level importance, feature-dependence diagnostics, near-optimal supplied models, prediction agreement, explanation disagreement, explicit permitted-claim labels.
code: explainer <- as_explainers(result, models = "main_model")$main_model  importance <- calculate_permutation_importance(   explainer,   n_repeats = 30,   seed = 2026 )  effect <- explain_effect(explainer, feature = "wt") audit <- audit_explanations(   as_explainers(comparison),   n_repeats = 30,   performance_tolerance = 0.05,   seed = 2026 )  audit render_explanation_report(audit, "technical-evidence.html")

what: A complete explanation never requires an LLM
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: deterministic local narrative always default—even API key present: Remote generation requires explicit provider. aggregate metrics, feature names, warnings, provenance enter prompt; raw rows, fitted objects, case-level predictions, secrets . Current defaults documented official Gemini free-tier pricing, Groq rate limits, Ollama local API, OpenRouter free routing. Provider, resolved model, fallback, prompt provenance retained.
code: memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result) render_model_report(result, "report.html", narrative = memo) narrative_providers()  memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "gemini") memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "groq") memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "ollama") memo <- generate_natural_language_report(result, provider = "openrouter")

what: Classification, existing models, and H2O use the same contract
title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: Regression predictions numeric vectors. Binary predictions positive-class probabilities. Multiclass predictions named probability matrices. package validates contract expensive explanation begins.
code: # Multiclass classification flowers <- autoxplain(iris, target_column = "Species", seed = 2026)  # Any existing model or framework existing <- explain_model(   fitted_object,   data = held_out_data,   y = "outcome",   task = "binary",   positive = "yes",   predict_function = function(model, newdata) {     # Return P(outcome == "yes") as a numeric vector.   } )  # Broader optional AutoML candidate set; requires Java and h2o h2o_result <- autoxplain(   training_data,   target_column = "outcome",   test_data = held_out_data,   engine = "h2o",   max_models = 10,   seed = 2026 )

title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: Held-performance describes supplied evaluation data, every future population time period. Pareto frontier covers supplied models displayed objectives. Permutation importance estimates fitted-model reliance sensitive dependent predictors. ALE reduces direct marginal extrapolation remains associational. Generated prose can wrong; numerical report remains authoritative. AutoXplainR automate causal claims, fairness certification, safety approval, deployment decisions.

title: Guided Model Fitting, Evaluation, and Explanation
text: AutoXplainR complements modeling explanation frameworks rather hiding . DALEX, ingredients, iml provide broad explanation ecosystems; xplainfi provides formal importance-inference tools; tidymodels, mlr3, H2O provide larger modeling systems. AutoXplainR’s role approachable contract joining fit, unseen-data evaluation, comparison, explanation reliability, careful communication. methods grounded Fisher, Rudin, Dominici’s model-class-reliance work, Apley Zhu’s ALE method, Biecek’s DALEX framework.

title: Convert an AutoML result to model-agnostic explainers — as_explainers
text: x autoxplain_result. data Optional raw evaluation data. fitted preprocessing recipe applied automatically. Defaults held-test_data available, otherwise training data. models Model indices, IDs, NULL retained models.

title: Convert an AutoML result to model-agnostic explainers — as_explainers
text: named list autoxplain_explainer objects.

what: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: audit_explanations() AutoXplainR's advanced reliability layer. evaluates repeated permutation importance, feature dependence, Monte Carlo stability, prediction disagreement, explanation disagreement among near-equivalent models. output distinguishes robust descriptive evidence claims need data conditional method.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
code: audit_explanations(   explainers,   features = NULL,   metric = "auto",   n_repeats = 20L,   seed = 123L,   confidence = 0.95,   performance_tolerance = 0.05,   dependence_threshold = 0.7 )

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: explainers autoxplain_explainer list explainers. features Features shared every explainer. Defaults intersection. metric Performance metric passed calculate_permutation_importance(). n_repeats Number permutations per model feature. seed Reproducible seed. confidence Monte Carlo interval level. performance_tolerance Relative tolerance defining empirical set near-optimal supplied models. example, 0.05 retains models whose evaluation score within five percent best supplied score. dependence_threshold Association marginal importance PDP claims receive dependence warning.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: object class autoxplain_audit.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: diagnostic protocol, formal certification substitute domain review, causal identification, external validation.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
code: train <- mtcars[1:24, ] test <- mtcars[25:32, ] lm1 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, train) lm2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, train) e1 <- explain_model(lm1, test, "mpg", label = "model A") e2 <- explain_model(lm2, test, "mpg", label = "model B") audit <- audit_explanations(list(e1, e2), n_repeats = 5) audit #> <AutoXplainR explanation evidence audit> #>   grade:              C (diagnostic, not certification) #>   models:             2 (1 near-optimal) #>   stable claims:      0.0% #>   max dependence:     0.929 #>   explanation accord: n/a (one model) #>   prediction accord:  n/a (one model) #>  #> Findings #>   [warning] 8 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #>   [warning] 20 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported. #>   [note] The permutation budget is suitable for smoke testing, not a final report. #>   [note] The audit cannot verify that explanation data are independent of model fitting.

what: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
text: autoxplain() beginner-first entry point. default creates reproducible held-split, fits intercept-baseline understandable statistical model, evaluates unseen rows, stores everything needed explanation reporting. path uses R require Java cloud account.

title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
code: autoxplain(   data,   target_column,   max_models = 10L,   max_runtime_secs = 300L,   seed = 123L,   test_data = NULL,   test_fraction = 0.2,   engine = c("auto", "base", "h2o"),   model_set = c("quick", "comparison"),   enable_preprocessing = TRUE,   preprocessing_config = list(),   task = c("auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass"),   nfolds = 5L,   sort_metric = "AUTO",   include_algos = NULL,   exclude_algos = NULL,   use_test_as_validation = FALSE,   init_h2o = TRUE,   h2o_nthreads = -1L,   h2o_max_mem_size = "2G",   verbosity = c("quiet", "info") )

title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
text: data Training data frame. target_column Name outcome column. max_models Maximum number H2O base models; ignored guided base engine, fits one primary model one simple baseline. max_runtime_secs H2O training time budget seconds; ignored guided base engine. seed Reproducible split, fitting, H2O seed. test_data Optional held-evaluation data. used H2O validation frame unless use_test_as_validation = TRUE. test_fraction Fraction data reserved evaluation test_data supplied. Classification splits stratified. engine One "auto", "base", "h2o". "auto" currently resolves dependency-free "base" workflow. model_set Guided base-engine candidates. "quick" fits pre-specified understandable model baseline. "comparison" also fits shallow flexible decision trees descriptive multi-model Pareto trade-view. Held-ranks silently replace pre-specified primary model. enable_preprocessing Apply preprocess_data(). preprocessing_config Named overrides preprocessing. Identifier removal defaults FALSE. task One "auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass". nfolds Number H2O cross-validation folds. least two folds required stacked ensembles desired. sort_metric H2O AutoML leaderboard metric. include_algos, exclude_algos Optional H2O algorithm filters. Supply one. use_test_as_validation Whether pass test_data H2O validation frame. default keeps held-explanation data independent. init_h2o Start local H2O cluster connection available. h2o_nthreads Threads used starting H2O. h2o_max_mem_size Memory used starting H2O. verbosity One "quiet" "info".

title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
text: autoxplain_result containing models, data-frame leaderboard, task metadata, preprocessing provenance, evaluation data.

title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
text: Set engine = "h2o" use optional H2O AutoML adapter. lower-level explain_model() interface accepts models fitted framework. Numeric outcomes exactly two distinct values treated binary classification default. Potentially destructive preprocessing, identifier removal, opt-recorded result.

title: Fit and evaluate a model through a guided workflow — autoxplain
code: result <- autoxplain(mtcars, "mpg") result #> <AutoXplainR guided result> #>   question:   predict `mpg` (regression) #>   engine:     base #>   data:       26 training + 6 evaluation rows #>   models:     2 (primary + baseline) #>   result:     primary model has rmse = 2.4413 #>   baseline:   63.1% improvement in rmse #>   next:       use as_explainers() to investigate the fitted patterns explainers <- as_explainers(result) audit_explanations(explainers) #> <AutoXplainR explanation evidence audit> #>   grade:              C (diagnostic, not certification) #>   models:             2 (1 near-optimal) #>   stable claims:      0.0% #>   max dependence:     0.949 #>   explanation accord: n/a (one model) #>   prediction accord:  n/a (one model) #>  #> Findings #>   [warning] 10 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #>   [warning] 20 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported.  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ h2o_result <- autoxplain(mtcars, "mpg", engine = "h2o", max_models = 3) } # }

title: Calculate partial dependence data — calculate_partial_dependence
text: model autoxplain_explainer fitted model. data Reference data fitted model. explainer, defaults stored feature data. feature single feature name. n_points Number quantile bins ALE grid points PDP. quantile_range Numeric length-two range used numeric PDP grid. return_all_classes Retained compatibility. Multiclass callers make separate class-specific explainers; TRUE supported ALE. grid_size Deprecated alias n_points. sample_size Maximum number reference rows used PDP. NULL uses rows. what: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
title: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
text: Measures change --sample performance jointly permuting one feature (user-defined feature group). Unlike single permutation bar chart, result retains every repeat, Monte Carlo uncertainty, sign stability, evaluation contract required explanation audit.

title: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
code: calculate_permutation_importance(   model,   data = NULL,   target_column = NULL,   metric = "auto",   n_repeats = 20L,   seed = 123L,   features = NULL,   feature_groups = NULL,   within = NULL,   confidence = 0.95,   predict_function = NULL,   task = "auto",   positive = NULL,   n_permutations = NULL )

title: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
text: model autoxplain_explainer fitted model. data Evaluation data. Required fitted model; ignored model explainer unless supplied replace evaluation data. target_column Outcome column name model fitted model. metric One "auto", "rmse", "mae", "logloss", "accuracy", "auc". n_repeats Number independent permutations. seed Random seed. caller's random-number state restored. features Character vector features evaluate. Defaults . feature_groups Optional named list character vectors. Features element permuted together, useful one-hot encodings semantically coupled predictors. within Optional character vector naming columns define strata, vector/factor one stratum per row. Permutations occur within strata. blocked sensitivity analysis, general conditional permutation importance estimator. confidence Confidence level Monte Carlo intervals. predict_function Optional prediction function fitted model. task Task type passed explain_model(). positive Positive class passed explain_model(). n_permutations Deprecated alias n_repeats.

title: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
text: data frame class autoxplain_importance repeat-level values stored repeat_scores attribute.

title: Repeated permutation feature importance with uncertainty diagnostics — calculate_permutation_importance
text: confidence limits quantify Monte Carlo variation across permutations. confidence intervals population-level variable importance. Use genuinely held-evaluation set formal inference method population claims required.

text: model fitted model object. data data frame. may contain outcome y name column; outcome removed feature data stored explainer. y Outcome values, single column name data. predict_function Optional prediction function. may signature function(model, newdata) function(newdata). Regression functions return numeric vector. Classification functions return probabilities (vector binary outcomes matrix/data frame one column per class). task One "auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass". label Human-readable model label. positive Positive outcome level binary classification. default second outcome level used. metadata Optional named list recorded explainer provenance.

title: Generate a guided model report — generate_dashboard
text: autoxplain_result autoxplain_result. output_file Destination HTML file. top_features Maximum number features included audit. initial ranking obtained repeated permutation importance leading model, never impurity importance. sample_instances Retained backward compatibility; longer used. include_llm_report Whether request optional narrative. evidence report remains complete without . narrative_provider Provider passed generate_natural_language_report() include_llm_report = TRUE. defaults deterministic local provider. narrative_args Optional named arguments forwarded generate_natural_language_report(). open_browser Open result interactively. performance_weight Deprecated ignored. Performance explanation reliability reported separately rather collapsed arbitrary weighted score. n_repeats Permutation repeats final audit. max_models Maximum retained models compare.

title: Generate an evidence-constrained narrative — generate_natural_language_report
text: Produces deterministic local narrative default. Remote locally hosted generative models used provider set explicitly. Raw rows, fitted objects, case-level predictions, secrets never included prompt. Generated prose remains secondary computed evaluation explanation evidence.

title: Generate an evidence-constrained narrative — generate_natural_language_report
code: generate_natural_language_report(   autoxplain_result,   importance_data = NULL,   pdp_data = NULL,   model_characteristics = NULL,   audit = NULL,   provider = c("local", "gemini", "groq", "ollama", "openrouter", "custom"),   api_key = NULL,   model = NULL,   base_url = NULL,   max_tokens = 1000L,   temperature = 0.2,   timeout = 30,   fallback = TRUE,   use_remote = NULL,   transport = NULL )

title: Generate an evidence-constrained narrative — generate_natural_language_report
text: autoxplain_result autoxplain_result autoxplain_audit. importance_data Optional permutation importance data. pdp_data Optional feature-effect list. model_characteristics Optional model metadata. audit Optional autoxplain_audit. provider One "local", "gemini", "groq", "ollama", "openrouter", "custom". default always "local". api_key Provider API key. explicitly selected hosted provider, corresponding environment variable shown narrative_providers() consulted NULL. model Model identifier. NULL uses provider default shown narrative_providers(). custom provider requires explicit model. base_url Optional endpoint override. custom provider requires . max_tokens Maximum response tokens. temperature Sampling temperature. timeout Request timeout seconds. fallback Return deterministic narrative remote call fails. use_remote Deprecated compatibility switch. FALSE forces local generation; TRUE explicit provider selects Gemini. Prefer provider. transport Optional advanced request function testing custom networking. receives one request list must return narrative text.

title: Generate an evidence-constrained narrative — generate_natural_language_report
text: single character string narrative_provenance attribute.

title: Render a standalone explanation evidence report — render_explanation_report
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars) x <- explain_model(fit, mtcars, "mpg") audit <- audit_explanations(x, n_repeats = 3) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") render_explanation_report(audit, path) unlink(path)

what: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
title: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
text: Creates standalone HTML report autoxplain() result. report leads prediction question, held-evaluation, simple-baseline comparison, plain metric definitions. Feature reliance, fitted effects, explanation evidence audit follow progressively detail.

title: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
code: render_model_report(   result,   output_file = "autoxplain-report.html",   title = NULL,   audit = NULL,   effects = NULL,   narrative = NULL,   open = FALSE,   top_features = 8L,   n_repeats = 20L,   max_models = 5L )

title: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
text: result autoxplain_result. output_file Destination .html path. title Optional report title. NULL uses target name. audit Optional precomputed autoxplain_audit. effects Optional named list feature-effect objects. narrative Optional narrative returned generate_natural_language_report(). open Open report browser writing . top_features Maximum number features audited audit supplied. n_repeats Permutation repeats audit supplied. max_models Maximum models audited audit supplied.

title: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
text: normalized output path, invisibly.

title: Render a beginner-first model report — render_model_report
code: result <- autoxplain(mtcars, "mpg", seed = 2026) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") render_model_report(result, path, n_repeats = 3) unlink(path)

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Added dependency-light autoxplain(data, target) path creates reproducible holdout, fits simple baseline understandable primary model, evaluates task-appropriate metrics definitions. Added local linear, logistic, multinomial logistic workflows regression, binary classification, multiclass classification. H2O remains available explicitly engine = "h2o". Added render_model_report(), standalone beginner-first report leads modeling question, held-baseline comparison, metric definitions revealing feature patterns collapsed evidence audit. Added evaluation-row predictions, regression error diagnostics, classification confusion matrices, Brier scores, actionable warnings sample-size baseline-performance failure modes. guided workflow now rejects unsupported non-finite inputs, removes records constant predictors, reapplies fitted preprocessing recipe raw evaluation data supplied later. Added model_set = "comparison", dependency-light candidate set pre-specified statistical model, two decision-tree complexities, simple baseline. Held-candidate ranks remain descriptive rather silently replacing primary model. Added candidate-set-relative Pareto analysis model_tradeoffs() rebuilt plot_model_comparison() around explicit performance-versus-size trade-offs rather subjective composite score. Guided reports now include plain-language multi-model trade-section dependency-free Pareto SVG whenever two candidates fitted. Numerical fit warnings captured model diagnostics translated actionable report warning instead lost printed without context. Repositioned model-agnostic explanation evidence audit advanced reliability layer behind beginner workflow. Added explain_model() validated prediction contract regression, binary classification, multiclass classification. Added audit_explanations() combine repeated-importance stability, feature dependence, near-optimal-model importance ranges, prediction agreement, prioritized findings, explicit permitted-claim labels. Added render_explanation_report(), standalone accessible HTML artifact runtime dependency H2O, Plotly, Flexdashboard, LLM.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Rebuilt permutation importance retain repeat-level values, sign stability, grouped-feature permutations, optional blocked permutations, Monte Carlo intervals whose interpretation stated explicitly. Added accumulated local effects (ALE) default feature-effect method. Rebuilt PDP estimation relative support predictor-dependence diagnostics. Added supplied-model Rashomon diagnostics: empirical near-optimal membership, feature-importance ranges, rank agreement, prediction ambiguity. Numeric two-level outcomes now detected binary classification instead silently becoming regression targets.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: H2O AutoML now optional fitting adapter; core audits use lightweight runtime dependency set. Test data remain held H2O validation default. Preprocessing conservative, returns reusable recipe, applies fitted levels/imputation values evaluation data. Identifier removal guessed ordinal conversion opt-. Added engine-neutral preprocess_data() name; preprocess_for_h2o() retained compatibility alias. Replaced generated 2,300-line Flexdashboard implementation evidence-report pipeline used model-agnostic callers. Replaced fixed, ranking-first LLM prompts local deterministic reports explicit aggregated-data-Gemini, Groq, Ollama, OpenRouter, custom OpenAI-compatible adapters. Local deterministic reporting always default, even API key present, provider/model/fallback details attached provenance. Removed bundled third-party datasets whose provenance redistribution terms documented.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Added CRAN-oriented metadata, automated checks, coverage, pkgdown deployment, issue forms, contribution security policies, citation metadata, release checklist. Enforced 80% statement-coverage floor exercising Plotly H2O integration paths; full local suite currently measures floor. Split fast model-agnostic unit tests opt-H2O integration tests. Rebuilt examples vignette around held-evaluation explicit interpretation boundaries.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.2
text: Added min-max weighted model score. Added performance_weight legacy dashboard.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.1
text: Expanded legacy dashboard Gemini integration.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.0
text: Initial experimental H2O AutoML release.
